results.title: Rick Rule and Sprott: Full History of His Role at Sprott Asset Management | Canadian Mining Report
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > From the 2011 acquisition of Global Resource Investments to his retirement as Senior Managi...
Content:
On September 22, 2010, Sprott Inc. (TSX: SII) announced an agreement to acquire Global Resource Investments, Terra Resource Investment Management, and Resource Capital Investments from Rick Rule and the Rule Family Trust. The deal was valued at approximately $90 million, paid primarily in Sprott shares (20 million common shares issued).
The definitive agreement was signed on January 24, 2011, and the transaction closed in February 2011. Reuters reported on January 24, 2011: “Sprott signs agreement with Rick Rule to buy US group.” The acquisition gave Sprott a stronger U.S. presence and brought Rule’s expertise and client relationships into the firm.

Section Title: Sprott to swap $90 million worth of its stock to acquire Rule Investments
Content:
Brokerage deal will allow Sprott to leverage its products and brands in the U.S. and internationally
By: [The Canadian Press](https://www.investmentexecutive.com/writer/canadian-press/)
September 22, 2010 September 22, 2010
15:45
Share
Facebook
LinkedIn
Twitter
Mail to a fried
Print
The Canadian Press
*Source: The Canadian Press*
Sprott Inc. stock jumped Wednesday after the investment firm (TSX:SII) announced it has agreed to acquire a California-based, resource-oriented financial services firm that manages US$1.3 billion of client assets.
The Toronto-based company’s shares gained 9% on Wednesday, pushing up the value of an all-stock deal that was worth C$90 million to C$125 million when it was announced before markets opened.
 ... 
Section Title: Sprott to swap $90 million worth of its stock to acquire Rule Investments
Content:
“We would have loved to have done this deal for cash a few months ago but Rick, right from the start, told us he was interested in being a partner in our business. So this is a lot more like a merger than an acquisition from the perspective,” Sprott chief executive Peter Grosskopf said on the call.
The shares closed at $4.50 each on Tuesday, making the offer initially worth about $90 million at closing and up to $125 million if the maximum number of shares are issued.
However, the value of the offer will fluctuate with the price of Sprott shares. They gained 40 cents or 8.9% on the Toronto Stock Exchange Wednesday, although with modest volume of about 250,000 shares.

results.title: Sprott signs agreement with Rick Rule to buy US group | Reuters
excerpts:
Section Title: Sprott signs agreement with Rick Rule to buy US group
Content:
By Reuters
Summary
Agreement signed with Rick Rule, Rule Family Trust
Deal expected to close in Feb
Jan 24 (Reuters) - Canadian fund manager Sprott Inc's said it signed an agreement with Rick Rule and the Rule Family Trust to buy three U.S.-based resource investment companies as part of its expansion plans.
The company, which had announced its plans to buy Global Resource Investments, Terra Resource Investment Management and Resource Capital Investments on Sept. 22 last year, said it expects the deal to close in February. [ID:nSGE68L0I5]
Sign up here.
Sprott's targets are led by Rick Rule, a U.S.-based investor specializing in mining and energy.
Shares of the Toronto-based company closed at C$8.50 on the Toronto Stock Exchange.
Reporting by Amruta Sabnis in Bangalore

results.title: Rick Rule - Stansberry Conferences
excerpts:
Section Title: Rick Rule > Founder & CEO, Rule Investment Media
Content:
Rick Rule began his career in 1974 in the securities business and has been involved in it ever since. He is known for his expertise in many resource sectors, including agriculture, alternative energy, forestry, oil and gas, mining, and water. In 1990, Rick founded the business now known as Sprott US Holdings, merging it into Sprott (SII) in 2011.
He retired from Sprott in 2021, and resigned from the board of directors in 2023, but remains Sprott’s largest shareholder. Rick is celebrating his retirement by co-founding a new bank, Battle Bank. Rick is also actively engaged in private-placement markets through originating and participating in hundreds of debt and equity transactions.

Section Title: Sprott: 2025 Was A Fantastic Year For The Company, But Cash Flow Generation Lags
Content:
Jan. 05, 2026 1:46 PM ET Sprott Inc. (SII) Stock , SII:CA Stock SLVR
Bang For The Buck
Investing Group Leader
Follow
5Share
Save
Play (8min)
Comments (8)
Section Title: Sprott: 2025 Was A Fantastic Year For The Company, But Cash Flow Generation Lags > Summary
Content:
Sprott Inc. has demonstrated robust AUM growth, reaching $49B in Q3 2025, driven by strong precious metals prices and healthy inflows.
The Exchange Listed Products segment, now 85% of AUM, remains the primary growth engine, with notable ETF launches and accelerating inflows, especially in Physical Trusts.
Despite 189% AUM growth since 2021, SII's earnings and cash flow have lagged, with adjusted EBITDA heavily impacted by stock-based compensation adjustments.
I rate SII as a Hold due to a sub-3% free cash flow yield and a forward P/E of ~30, reflecting insufficient margin of safety despite acquisition speculation.
Looking for more investing ideas like this one? Get them exclusively at Off The Beaten Path. Learn More »
Alex Cristi /iStock via Getty Images
